model_narrative: We plan a network of warehouses that serve a number of demand regions. We decide which warehouses to open and, for each region, which open warehouse will serve it. Opening a warehouse incurs a fixed cost, and serving a region from a warehouse incurs a transportation cost. We also track the resulting total fixed cost, total transportation cost, and overall total cost. The objective is to minimize the overall total cost.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of candidate warehouses that may be opened

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of demand regions that must each be served

params.name: f
params.doc: the fixed cost incurred to open each warehouse, in cost units

params.name: c
params.doc: the capacity of each warehouse, expressed in the same units as region demand

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand of each region, expressed in the same units as warehouse capacity

params.name: t
params.doc: the transportation cost of serving a region from a warehouse, in cost units; indexed by region first and warehouse second

vars.name: totcost
vars.doc: the total cost of the chosen plan, in cost units

vars.name: fcost
vars.doc: the total fixed cost of opening warehouses, in cost units

vars.name: tcost
vars.doc: the total transportation cost of serving regions, in cost units

vars.name: ow
vars.doc: warehouse-open indicator; equals 1 if the warehouse is opened and 0 otherwise

vars.name: oa
vars.doc: shipment-arc indicator; equals 1 if the region is served from the warehouse and 0 otherwise

constraints.name: deftotcost
constraints.intent: The total cost must equal the fixed cost of the warehouses opened plus the transportation cost of serving the regions.

constraints.name: deffcost
constraints.intent: The total fixed cost must equal the sum, over all warehouses, of each warehouse's fixed opening cost counted only when that warehouse is opened.

constraints.name: deftcost
constraints.intent: The total transportation cost must equal the sum, over every region and warehouse pairing, of the cost of serving that region from that warehouse counted only when the region is actually served from it.

constraints.name: defwcap
constraints.intent: For each warehouse, the combined demand of all regions it serves cannot exceed that warehouse's capacity.

constraints.name: onew
constraints.intent: Each region must be served by exactly one warehouse.

constraints.name: defow
constraints.intent: A region can be served from a warehouse only if that warehouse is open, so whenever a region is served from a warehouse that warehouse must be open.

query: There's a proposal on the table to rebalance our two hubs so the smaller site isn't perpetually the lighter one. We'd require warehouse 1 to carry at least as much total served demand as warehouse 2 — its volume, measured in units shipped across all its regions, never falling below warehouse 2's. Plug that in and tell me what the all-in cost becomes.
expected_pyomo: model.balance_w1_w2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.d[j] * model.oa['w1', j] for j in model.j) >= sum(model.d[j] * model.oa['w2', j] for j in model.j))

query: Continuity planning is floating a rule that we never run on fewer than three live sites, so one closure can't strand a region. Try requiring at least three warehouses open and tell me the resulting total cost.
expected_pyomo: model.min_open = Constraint(expr=sum(model.ow[i] for i in model.i) >= 3)

query: We're looking at a contingency where region 8 has to be served out of one of our two larger northern sites — warehouse 3 or warehouse 4 — rather than the close-in hubs, in case those are reserved for other freight. Require region 8's delivery to land on at least one of those two sites. What does the optimizer return for the total cost?

query: The plan runs our open sites right up near the edge of their rated space, which leaves us no cushion when a region's volume spikes. Shouldn't every warehouse hold its assigned load to no more than seventy percent of its own capacity, so there's headroom everywhere? What's the model seeing that makes it pack them tighter than that?

query: Operations is pushing back on the current plan on two fronts at once: warehouse 2 is shouldering region 8 on top of everything else, and warehouse 1 is fanned out across too many territories to manage cleanly. Couldn't we just pull region 8 off warehouse 2 entirely and at the same time keep warehouse 1 down to no more than three regions? What's the combined tradeoff that's pulling the plan the other way?
expected_pyomo:
model.r8_off_w2 = Constraint(expr=model.oa['w2', 'r8'] <= 0)
model.w1_span = Constraint(expr=sum(model.oa['w1', j] for j in model.j) <= 3)

query: We're carrying fixed overhead on two separate warehouses. Couldn't we just consolidate the whole operation into one site and wipe out a chunk of that cost? Walk me through what keeps the plan from collapsing to a single warehouse.
expected_pyomo: model.single_site = Constraint(expr=sum(model.ow[i] for i in model.i) <= 1)
